arts.sum: The Seagulls haven’t tasted victory for seven games heading into this fixture, but have lost just once at home in the league, meaning we’ll have to be as red-hot as we have in recent weeks to avoid freezing on the south coast. We have won three of our last five Premier League games against Brighton, as many as we had in their first 10 against the Seagulls in the competition (D3 L4).

arts.ttl: Brighton star set to be out of action for 3 months – Not the best news for Seagulls
arts.sum: According to a prominent Turkish journalist, Brighton & Hove Albion defender Ferdi Kadıoğlu is facing a lengthy spell on the sidelines. The Turkish international signed for the Premier League side in the summer from Fenerbahce, but his time in England has been blighted by injury. arts.ttl: Gabriel Jesus on eight years in the Premier League
arts.sum: “It has been a long time since I came to the Premier League,” he said on Friday. Jesus is of course no stranger to lifting the Premier League title, having done so four times during his spell at Man City.

arts.ttl: Evan Ferguson odds for Brighton exit plummet with Arsenal favourites to sign Irishman in January transfer window
arts.sum: The 20-year-old could be leaving Brighton in the January transfer window, as odds slash to just 2/1 to join the Gunners.

arts.ttl: Celtic in pre-contract talks with Arsenal's Tierney
arts.sum: Celtic hold talks to re-sign former defender Kieran Tierney on a pre-contract deal from Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta explains new Declan Rice plan after eye-catching Arsenal omissions
arts.sum: Declan Rice has seen his game-time decrease over the last month, with Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta frequently subbing him off or dropping him down to the bench over the festive period

arts.ttl: Arsenal hold Ayden Heaven contract talks as Gunners look to keep wonderkid
arts.sum: Youngster has trained with Mikel Arteta’s first-team regularly this season

arts.ttl: Arsenal and Chelsea face SHOCK competition for €100m Brazilian wonderkid
arts.sum: Brighton & Hove Albion have reportedly submitted a third bid for highly-rated Palmeiras defender Vitor Reis as they look to fend off significant competition for his signature. Furthermore, the Seagulls have now submitted a third bid worth €28.3 million (£23.5m) for Reis, according to The Express, and they are hopeful that their latest offer will tempt Palmeiras into a January sale.

arts.ttl: Kieran Tierney Celtic return announcement plan 'set' – key contract details emerge as Arsenal endgame arrives
arts.sum: Tierney looks set to be a Celtic player again – and here's when it could happen

arts.ttl: Sources: How Sunderland now feel about selling Jobe Bellingham as Arsenal send scouts to Stadium of Light
